top_signals.title: UK manufacturers facing ‘steel quota cliff edge’
top_signals.summary: Manufacturers are facing a cliff-edge from the government’s incoming steel tariffs that are likely to inflict “huge damage” on the UK economy, Britain’s largest industry body has warned. In a fresh statement, the British Chambers of Commerce (BCC) sounded the alarm on the impact that that new duties on foreign-produced steel will have on importers, [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Barclays sees gold pullback as ‘reset’, keeps bullish outlook
top_signals.summary: According to the bank, the recent correction has not altered the key drivers underpinning the gold bull market.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Resouro PEA points to $1B potential rare earth and titanium project
top_signals.summary: The Tiros project in Minas Gerais, Brazil, hosts 1.4 billion tonnes in the measured and indicated categories.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Rare-earth refiner gets $500M loan from Pentagon to build ‘Freedom Facility’
top_signals.summary: The commitment is part of a roughly $1 billion initiative to expand US capacity to separate and produce rare earths.
